steps.title: Spot the goal: making a generalization
steps.content: The student wants to make a generalization—a broader claim about exoplanets as a category—supported by evidence from the notes, not just facts about individual exoplanets.

steps.title: Apply the requirement to the choices
steps.content: Choice D makes a generalization about exoplanet names—they have diverse origins—and supports it directly with examples from the notes: Phobetor (Greek mythology) and Mastika (Malay) illustrate different origins. The remaining choices either describe facts without forming a broader generalization or focus on stars instead of exoplanets. This matches choice D — D is correct.
